claims.subject: Lazada Singapore
claims.evidence: lazada was pricing, the contract came up for renewal and our pricing wasn't competitive against a cheaper local competitor for the volume tier they were in
claims.object_value: pricing — not competitive against cheaper local competitor for volume tier

claims.subject: Cosmos Hotels
claims.evidence: cosmos hotels we lost to a competitor — not on price, on product, they wanted multi-property reporting that we don't have yet
claims.object_value: lost to competitor on product — wanted multi-property reporting not available

claims.subject: Banco Verde
claims.evidence: banco verde was a internal bank decision, they're consolidating payment vendors as part of their own restructuring, that one is not really about us
claims.object_value: internal bank restructuring — consolidating payment vendors

claims.subject: HelixPay
claims.evidence: the gap is qualitative signal — the things csms hear in calls don't always make it back into the system.
claims.object_value: qualitative signal from CSM calls not consistently captured in Gainsight

claims.subject: HelixPay
claims.evidence: right now if maria santos's team hears 'we hate the reconciliation report' from twelve customers, that becomes a slack message to beatriz, beatriz adds it to a backlog, eventually it gets prioritized or doesn't, and the csm never knows whether they were heard.
claims.object_value: product feedback from CSMs goes to Slack message to Beatriz, added to backlog, CSM never notified of outcome

claims.subject: HelixPay
claims.evidence: the all-hands tone last week was more upbeat than the weekly review tone. that's not unusual but candidates for the codos work should know that what people hear in all-hands is not the full picture.
claims.object_value: all-hands tone more upbeat than weekly review reality; NPS gap, Brazil tier-2 churn risk, and Brazil support response times are known to Wei and Priya but not fully surfaced in all-hands
